summary: Task 03 [012] is implemented exactly per spec (Decision 3 + Decision 5). pretty.SizeWidth is a clean one-line wrapper over Size using fmt.Sprintf("%*s", width, Size(v)) — it never truncates, mirrors ReserveWidth, and does not touch Size. sizeFieldWidth=8 is a named const with documented rationale; all five verbose Size fields use SizeWidth and their n/a fallbacks use naReserve(sizeFieldWidth) so value and n/a share the reserve. The wal-size field is correctly left as bare pretty.Size. Displayed digits/units are unchanged (only leading padding added), goldens updated accordingly, tests pass, and code is idiomatic with no builtin shadowing. No tech debt introduced.

checks.sizeWidth_thin_wrapper: pass — fmt.Sprintf("%*s", width, Size(v)), no duplicated switch
checks.sizeWidth_never_truncates: pass — %*s widens; TestSizeWidth asserts len(got) >= width and Size digits preserved via TrimLeft
checks.sizeFieldWidth_named_const_with_rational: pass — const sizeFieldWidth = 8 near cacheHitWidth with widest-realistic-7-chars rationale
checks.all_five_verbose_fields_use_SizeWidth: pass — TotalSize, GrowthPerSec, LagBytes, RetainedBytes, ArchivingBacklog
checks.na_fallbacks_use_naReserve: pass — all five init/fallbacks use naReserve(sizeFieldWidth); naReserve floors at len(naLiteral)=3, 8>3 so width preserved
checks.numbers_units_unchanged: pass — only leading padding added; goldens differ only by leading spaces; tests green
